pr.title: Use `.textContent` to get text from the `#ctx-text` element
pr.author: NateXVI

summary: Fixed the generated context-page copy button by reading `#ctx-text` with DOM `.textContent` instead of `.innerText`. The bug appeared when the target assembly text was collapsed: on Chrome/Windows, `innerText` could return an empty string because it reflects rendered/visible selectable text, while `textContent` returns the underlying node content regardless of collapsed display state. This made the docs/getting-started workflow more reliable for copying target assembly context.
what_changed: In `tools/ctx_template.html`, changed the copy-button handler from `document.querySelector('#ctx-text').innerText.trim()` to `document.querySelector('#ctx-text').textContent.trim()`.
The surrounding behavior remained the same: after obtaining the text, the page still writes it to the clipboard with `navigator.clipboard.writeText(text)` and logs success/failure.
The fix targeted the context page linked from getting-started instructions, specifically the copy button for target assembly/context text.
smart_moves: Used the DOM property that matches the intended data source. The copy action needs the full underlying text payload, not the browser's approximation of visibly selectable rendered text.
Kept the patch minimal: a one-line substitution avoided changing UI behavior, clipboard flow, or the generated template structure.
The report was grounded in user-observed workflow failure: following getting-started instructions, the copy button appeared broken when the text was collapsed.
Identified a cross-browser/layout-sensitive distinction: `innerText` can depend on rendered visibility, while `textContent` is stable for hidden or collapsed content.
decomp_lessons: For decomp support tooling and generated documentation pages, copy buttons should use source/content-oriented DOM APIs when the payload is code, assembly, or context text.
Avoid `innerText` for machine-consumed snippets that may be hidden, collapsed, styled, or otherwise not fully rendered; use `textContent` when the exact textual node contents are needed.
Small documentation/tooling bugs can block onboarding: the context-copy workflow is part of getting started, so reliability across common browser/OS combinations matters.
When browser behavior differs, prefer APIs with semantics closest to the intended invariant rather than adding browser-specific checks.
naming_conventions: Existing DOM IDs and variable names were preserved: `copy-button`, `ctx-text`, `copyButton`, and `text`.
No C symbol, struct, header, or decomp naming conventions were affected.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactic was changed in the codebase itself.
The indirect decomp relevance is that reliable copying of target assembly/context text supports setup and analysis workflows.
review_feedback: ribbanya noted that the existing `master` behavior still copied collapsed text on Firefox on X11 Linux and asked what browser was affected.
NateXVI replied that the failure occurred on Chrome on Windows 10.
There were no inline review comments in the provided slice.
follow_up_queries: Search for other tooling or docs templates using `.innerText` to copy hidden/collapsed code or assembly snippets.
Check generated `ctx.html` behavior in current docs across Chrome, Firefox, Windows, Linux, and macOS.
Look for other onboarding blockers in getting-started documentation where browser-specific behavior could affect decomp workflow.
